units.title: Well Done, Explorers! (Starter)
units.grammar: Revision of past simple regular and irregular. / Could/Couldn't.

units.title: In the Museum
units.grammar: Dinosaurs lived millions of years ago. / They didn't fly. / Did they eat meat? / Past simple review
key_phrases: They lived millions of years ago.

units.title: The World of Sports
units.grammar: I was playing tennis. / They were swimming. / Past continuous (was/were + V-ing)
key_phrases: While I was running, I fell.

units.title: Danger!
units.topic: Dangerous situations, must/mustn't, past continuous vs simple
units.grammar: You must wear a helmet. / You mustn't swim alone. / While I was walking, a dog bit me.

units.title: Two Return Tickets
units.grammar: The train will arrive at 3pm. / I think it will rain. / Will for predictions
key_phrases: I'd like two return tickets, please.
The bus will be here soon.
I think we'll miss the train.

units.title: Police!
units.grammar: Where were you at 8pm? / What did you see? / Was he wearing a mask? / Past simple questions
key_phrases: I was at home watching TV.
units.clil: Social Science: How police solve crimes

units.title: Mythical Beasts
units.grammar: There was a dragon. / There were two unicorns. / It had wings and a tail.
key_phrases: Long ago, there was a dragon.

units.title: Orchestra!
units.topic: Music, instruments, present perfect for experience
units.grammar: I've played the piano. / She's never sung in public. / Have you ever...? / Present perfect
key_phrases: Have you ever played the guitar?

units.title: In the Planetarium
units.grammar: I will go to Mars one day. / We won't live on the Moon. / Will people travel to space?
key_phrases: I think I'll be an astronaut.

units.title: At the Castle
units.grammar: People used to live in castles. / Knights used to wear armor. / They didn't use to have electricity.
key_phrases: There used to be a castle here.
People didn't use to have phones.
I used to believe in dragons.
